methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Curio Solutions is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Ed McGinnis and Pete Zweifel. The target operates within a frontier nuclear sector represented by 3 public comps (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-Progression valuation is the applicable methodology here. Using Curio's $16M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement yields $32M. This method is suitable given the company's defined stage and capital history. Peer-Median triangulation was not feasible—while 8 same-sector peers exist, the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) returned insufficient datapoints (fewer than 3 required for statistical reliability), and a broader sector median would introduce material distortion.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $27M to $37M, centered on $32M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the stage-progression point estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable-stage peer valuations in nuclear energy, which limits cross-validation, and the inherent volatility of frontier-tech capital structures. The tight comp set underscores execution risk and technology differentiation as primary value drivers warranting deeper operational diligence.
